Additional Features
MathJax
Underscore
Link
Open in New Tab

MathJax Example

In this modified version of jemdoc, a LaTeX equation block can be typed in as

∇⋅D=ρf∇⋅B=0,∇×E=−∂B∂t∇×H=Jf−∂D∂t. \nabla \cdot \mathbf{D} = \rho_f\\ \nabla \cdot \mathbf{B} = 0,\\ \nabla \times \mathbf{E} = -\frac{\partial\mathbf{B}}{\partial t}\\ \nabla \times \mathbf{H} = \mathbf{J}_f - \frac{\partial\mathbf{D}}{\partial t}. ∇⋅D=ρf​∇⋅B=0,∇×E=−∂t∂B​∇×H=Jf​−∂t∂D​.

You can also align equations using LaTeX's aligned block:

∇⋅D=ρf∇⋅B=0∇×E=−∂B∂t∇×H=Jf−∂D∂t. \begin{aligned} \nabla \cdot \mathbf{D} &= \rho_f\\ \nabla \cdot \mathbf{B} &= 0\\ \nabla \times \mathbf{E} &= -\frac{\partial\mathbf{B}}{\partial t}\\ \nabla \times \mathbf{H} &= \mathbf{J}_f - \frac{\partial\mathbf{D}}{\partial t}. \end{aligned} ∇⋅D∇⋅B∇×E∇×H​=ρf​=0=−∂t∂B​=Jf​−∂t∂D​.​

To number the equation block, wrap the equations with LaTeX's equation block:

∇⋅D=ρf∇⋅B=0∇×E=−∂B∂t∇×H=Jf−∂D∂t. \begin{equation} \begin{aligned} \nabla \cdot \mathbf{D} &= \rho_f\\ \nabla \cdot \mathbf{B} &= 0\\ \nabla \times \mathbf{E} &= -\frac{\partial\mathbf{B}}{\partial t}\\ \nabla \times \mathbf{H} &= \mathbf{J}_f - \frac{\partial\mathbf{D}}{\partial t}. \end{aligned} \end{equation} ∇⋅D∇⋅B∇×E∇×H​=ρf​=0=−∂t∂B​=Jf​−∂t∂D​.​​​

Instead, you can use LaTeX's align (not aligned) block to number the individual equations:

∇⋅D=ρf\labeleq:D∇⋅B=0\labeleq:B∇×E=−∂B∂t\labeleq:E∇×H=Jf−∂D∂t\labeleq:H \begin{align} \nabla \cdot \mathbf{D} &= \rho_f\label{eq:D}\\ \nabla \cdot \mathbf{B} &= 0\label{eq:B}\\ \nabla \times \mathbf{E} &= -\frac{\partial\mathbf{B}}{\partial t}\label{eq:E}\\ \nabla \times \mathbf{H} &= \mathbf{J}_f - \frac{\partial\mathbf{D}}{\partial t}\label{eq:H} \end{align} ∇⋅D∇⋅B∇×E∇×H​=ρf​\labeleq:D=0\labeleq:B=−∂t∂B​\labeleq:E=Jf​−∂t∂D​\labeleq:H​​

You can also type inline equations as Ax=bA x = bAx=b. Referencing equations is done as Eqs. \eqrefeq:E\eqref{eq:E}\eqrefeq:E and \eqrefeq:H\eqref{eq:H}\eqrefeq:H.
